source.sourcePath: raw/sources/Project/02_CYAN/4. 시험소 제출자료(601-1)/4. Risk management/A-FMEA-CYN_FMEA Table (Rev.0 250525).pdf

document.title: FMEA Report
document.documentType: FMEA report / risk management FMEA table

sourceSummary.oneLine: FMEA report for the Digital flat panel X-ray detector models GT1717C/GT1717CP under EN ISO 14971, IEC 60601-1, and EN 62366-1.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports risk management documentation by identifying hazards, risk control measures, verification evidence, and residual risk acceptability for GT1717C/GT1717CP.
keyPoints: Document identifies the product as Digital flat panel X-ray detector, model GT1717C/GT1717CP.
Revision No. is Rev. 0 with revision date May 25, 2025.

Essential performance includes intended image acquisition, no adjacent two-line or blank acquired image, normal operation when required, and DC power supplied through PoE injector and LAN cable during EMC testing.
Expected service life is approximately 5 years.
Residual risks are stated to be reduced to the acceptable region after risk control measures are implemented and verified.

entities.name: Digital flat panel X-ray detector
entities.evidence: Product Name : Digital flat panel X-ray detector

entities.name: A-RMR-CYN
entities.evidence: See Risk Management Report (Doc. No. A-RMR-CYN).

entities.name: A-LCR-CYN
entities.evidence: Refer to Life cycle report (Doc. No. A-LCR-CYN)

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Expected service life
concepts.evidence: The GT1717C/GT1717CP is designed for a service life of approximately 5years.

concepts.name: Risk control measures
concepts.evidence: As control measures for the identified risks, providing label and user manual, using certified battery, testing the device to meet IEC 60601-1, designing each cable type differently, establishing software development process in accordance with IEC 62304, radio-related testing, and using certified RF module, etc. were implemented and verified.

concepts.name: Overall residual risk acceptability
concepts.evidence: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting.

concepts.name: Software validation
concepts.evidence: Software development process was established in compliance with IEC 62304 and software validation was perform.

concepts.name: Electromagnetic compatibility
concepts.evidence: It was designed with the electromagnetic compatibility.

concepts.name: Use error risk control
concepts.evidence: A symbol to read the user manual appears on the label.

The GT1717C/GT1717CP expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: The GT1717C/GT1717CP is designed for a service life of approximately 5years.

evidence.claim: The document refers to a Risk Management Report with Doc. No. A-RMR-CYN.
evidence.evidenceText: See Risk Management Report (Doc. No. A-RMR-CYN).

evidence.claim: The document refers to a Life cycle report with Doc. No. A-LCR-CYN.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to Life cycle report (Doc. No. A-LCR-CYN)

evidence.claim: Essential performance includes acquiring the image as intended.
evidence.evidenceText: - Acquisition image is obtained as intended.

evidence.claim: Essential performance includes avoiding adjacent two-line or blank acquired images.
evidence.evidenceText: - The acquired image should not have two lines adjacent or be blank.

evidence.claim: The device uses a PoE injector and LAN cable for DC power.
evidence.evidenceText: The GT1717C/GT1717CP Use the POE injector and provide DC power via the LAN cable

evidence.claim: No battery is applied to GT1717C/GT1717CP.
evidence.evidenceText: No battery applied to GT1717C/GT1717CP

evidence.claim: The GT1717C/GT1717CP does not have an applied part.
evidence.evidenceText: The GT1717C/GT1717CP does not have a applied part.

evidence.claim: The GT1717C/GT1717CP does not have moving parts.
evidence.evidenceText: The GT1717C/GT1717CP does not have moving parts.

evidence.claim: The GT1717C/GT1717CP does not produce diagnostic or therapeutic radiation.
evidence.evidenceText: The GT1717C/GT1717CP does not produce diagnostic or therapeutic radiation.

evidence.claim: Software development process was established in compliance with IEC 62304 and software validation was performed.
evidence.evidenceText: Software risk management process was established in compliance with IEC 62304 and software validation was performed. Therefore there is no unacceptable risk.

evidence.claim: EMC risk control includes warning users not to use cellular phones or electric devices near the equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: A warning not to use of cellular phone or electric device near the equipment is described in user manual.

evidence.claim: After risk controls, residual risks are stated to be acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the residual risks within the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector are reduced in the acceptable region, and the new hazards needed for additional risk control measures are not identified.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text; authoritySignal is only 'general' metadata.
